v1_boundary: Milestone 3A validates the Saudi workbook and canonical invoice foundation only. No Saudi XML, QR, PDF/A-3, FATOORA submission, clearance, reporting, production cryptographic stamp or live-valid Saudi tax invoice claim.
v1_boundary_warning: Saudi V1 has not submitted to ZATCA/FATOORA, has not cleared standard B2B invoices, has not reported simplified invoices, has not applied a ZATCA clearance stamp and has not created production cryptographic signatures. Generated outputs are not live-valid Saudi B2B tax invoices.
legal_regime_summary: Saudi Arabia's ZATCA/FATOORA regime converts invoice issuing into structured electronic exchange through compliant e-invoicing solutions. Phase One covers generation requirements and Phase Two covers integration with ZATCA systems.
scope: Phase One generation requirements started on 4 December 2021.
Phase Two integration requirements started on 1 January 2023 and are rolled out by taxpayer waves.
The V1 workbench scenario is a Saudi standard B2B tax invoice.
mandatory_format: Formal Saudi e-invoice artefacts use the prescribed XML syntax or PDF/A-3 with embedded XML where applicable.
The invoice content must satisfy ZATCA data dictionary and XML implementation requirements.
Standard tax invoices and simplified tax invoices follow different authority workflows.
transmission_or_clearance_model: Standard B2B tax invoices follow the clearance model.
A standard B2B invoice must be submitted to ZATCA/FATOORA before it is shared with the buyer.
ZATCA validates the invoice and applies or returns the clearance stamp and QR update.
Simplified invoices follow a reporting model rather than the standard-invoice clearance model.
qr_signature_requirements: QR code content is required under the Saudi e-invoicing regime.
Production security features include cryptographic stamp/signature requirements.
The production previous-invoice-hash chain and certificate/CSID flows are outside this Milestone 1 app.
retention_or_audit_notes: The evidence bundle must retain the source upload, canonical invoice JSON, validation report, country pack manifest, hashes and clear V1 boundary warnings.
Official ZATCA SDK/toolbox or authority validation must remain not configured until a real validator or authority workflow is integrated.
v1_app_capability: Milestone 3A loads this pack, validates the Saudi workbook and canonical invoice JSON foundation and builds the local evidence bundle skeleton.
Saudi XML, Arabic/bilingual visual PDF, QR, clearance, reporting and production signing are intentionally not implemented in Milestone 3A.
This app does not submit, clear, report, obtain authority stamps or create production signatures.
boundary_highlights: Not a live-valid Saudi B2B tax invoice.
